steps.title: Identify the contradiction needed to refute the hypothesis
steps.content: The hypothesis claims larger bills appear in hotter habitats. To show this hypothesis isn't fully supported, we need data where the relationship reverses: a large bill in a cold habitat or vice versa.

steps.title: Match the reversal to the data
steps.content: D shows the largest-billed bird (ocellated, 3.26 cm²) in the coldest habitat (27.56°C) and the second-smallest-billed bird (Bendire's, 1.98 cm²) in the hottest habitat (36.57°C)—a direct reversal of the hypothesis. A and B contain factual errors about the temperature data; C shows a correlation that would actually support the hypothesis. This matches choice D — D is correct.
